member.name: T:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationMode
member.summary: Enumerates all supported authentication modes for repository connections. Determines how credentials are acquired and used for Azure or legacy resources.

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationMode.None
member.summary: No authentication is required/used (e.g. local Disk, InMemory providers).

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationMode.Legacy
member.summary: Uses legacy authentication with a direct connection string (classic). Includes AccountKey or SharedAccessKey in connection string.

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationMode.SasToken
member.summary: Uses Shared Access Signature (SAS) token for Azure Storage/Service Bus. Provides time-limited, granular access without sharing account keys.

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationMode.ClientSecret
member.summary: Uses Azure AD Client Secret credentials (ClientId + TenantId + Secret).

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationMode.ClientCertificate
member.summary: Uses Azure AD Client Certificate authentication (ClientId + TenantId + Certificate).

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationMode.DeviceCode
member.summary: Uses device code flow (user logs in via code provided in terminal/CLI).

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationMode.Interactive
member.summary: Uses interactive authentication via browser.

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationMode.ManagedIdentity
member.summary: Uses Azure Managed Identity (system- or user-assigned identity).

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationMode.WorkloadIdentity
member.summary: Uses federated OIDC workload identity (e.g. GitHub Actions, AKS Workload).

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationMode.SharedTokenCache
member.summary: Uses shared token cache (VS, Azure CLI, developer cache).

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationMode.DefaultAzureCredential
member.summary: Tries multiple credential sources in order (Azure Identity chaining). Order: Environment -> Managed Identity -> Visual Studio -> Azure CLI -> Azure PowerShell

member.name: T:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationOption
member.summary: Represents all authentication options for a repository connection. Supports both legacy (ConnectionString) and all modern Azure AD authentication modes.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationOption.Mode
member.summary: Specifies the authentication mode (how credentials are obtained).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationOption.ConnectionString
member.summary: Gets or sets the legacy connection string (for classic authentication). Should be null for modern Azure authentication.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationOption.SasToken
member.summary: Gets or sets the SAS (Shared Access Signature) token or full SAS URI. Used for time-limited, granular access to Azure Storage/Service Bus. Can be either: - Full SAS URI: "https://account.blob.core.windows.net/container?sv=...&sig=..." - SAS token only: "?sv=2021-06-08&ss=b&srt=sco&sp=rwdlac&sig=..."

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationOption.Token
member.summary: Gets or sets the acquired Azure AD token (for modern authentication).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationOption.CredentialProfile
member.summary: Gets or sets a custom name or ID of the credential profile used (optional). For developer scenarios (SharedTokenCache, VS, CLI, custom cache).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationOption.FullyQualifiedNamespace
member.summary: Gets or sets the fully qualified namespace for resources like Service Bus/Event Hub (optional). May be required by some authentication flows (modern Azure services).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationOption.TenantId
member.summary: Gets or sets the Azure AD Tenant ID.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationOption.ClientId
member.summary: Gets or sets the Azure AD Client ID (Application ID).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationOption.ClientSecret
member.summary: Gets or sets the Client Secret for ClientSecret authentication. Stored as SecureString to minimize plain-text exposure; converted only at the point of use.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationOption.CertificatePath
member.summary: Gets or sets the path to the certificate file for ClientCertificate authentication.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationOption.CertificatePassword
member.summary: Gets or sets the certificate password (if the certificate is password-protected). Stored as SecureString to minimize plain-text exposure; converted only at the point of use.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationOption.Certificate
member.summary: Gets or sets the X509Certificate2 instance directly (alternative to CertificatePath).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationOption.TokenFilePath
member.summary: Gets or sets the path to the federated token file for WorkloadIdentity authentication.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationOption.Username
member.summary: Gets or sets the username for SharedTokenCache authentication.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationOption.AuthorityHost
member.summary: Gets or sets the authority host (e.g., for sovereign clouds). Default is AzureAuthorityHosts.AzurePublicCloud.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationOption.ProxyUri
member.summary: Proxy URI for corporate proxy support (e.g., http://proxy.corp.local:8080). Required for on-premises environments accessing Azure through corporate proxy.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationOption.MaxRetries
member.summary: Maximum number of retry attempts for failed operations. Default: 3. Range: 1-10.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationOption.RetryDelaySeconds
member.summary: Delay in seconds between retry attempts. Default: 2 seconds. Range: 1-60 seconds.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationOption.MaxRetryDelaySeconds
member.summary: Maximum delay in seconds between retry attempts (for exponential backoff). Default: 30 seconds. Range: 5-300 seconds.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationOption.TimeoutSeconds
member.summary: Connection timeout in seconds. Default: 30 seconds. Range: 5-300 seconds.

member.name: T:PSDataRepository.Authentication.ResolvedAuth
member.summary: Discriminated result of authentication resolution. Eliminates duplicated 3-way auth dispatch across Azure provider constructors.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.ResolvedAuth.Kind
member.summary: The resolved authentication method.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.ResolvedAuth.ConnectionString
member.summary: Connection string (when Kind == ConnectionString).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.ResolvedAuth.SasToken
member.summary: SAS token or full SAS URI (when Kind == SasToken).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.ResolvedAuth.Credential
member.summary: Azure AD token credential (when Kind == TokenCredential).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Authentication.ResolvedAuth.IsSasFullUri
member.summary: True if the SAS token is a full URI (starts with http).

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Authentication.ResolvedAuth.BuildStorageSasUri(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Builds a storage SAS URI from account name, host suffix, and entity name. Ensures the SAS token starts with '?'.
member.returns: Full SAS URI.

member.name: T:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthKind
member.summary: Specifies the resolved authentication method.

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthKind.ConnectionString
member.summary: Legacy connection string with AccountKey or SharedAccessKey.

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthKind.SasToken
member.summary: Shared Access Signature token or URI.

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthKind.TokenCredential
member.summary: Azure AD token-based credential.

member.name: T:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AzureAuthResolver
member.summary: Centralizes the 3-way authentication resolution from into a single result. Used by all Azure provider constructors to eliminate duplicated auth dispatch logic.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Authentication.AzureAuthResolver.Resolve(PSDataRepository.Storage.StorageOption)
member.summary: Resolves the authentication from StorageOption into one of three modes: ConnectionString, SasToken, or TokenCredential.
member.returns: Resolved authentication result.

member.returns: The extracted storage account name.
member.exception: If account name cannot be extracted.

member.returns: A TokenCredential instance suitable for Azure SDK clients.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Authentication.CredentialFactory.CreateInteractiveCredential(PSDataRepository.Authentication.AuthenticationOption)

member.summary: Creates a DefaultAzureCredential which tries multiple credential sources in order. Order: Environment -> Managed Identity -> Visual Studio -> Azure CLI -> Azure PowerShell -> Interactive Browser All credential sources are enabled by default for maximum flexibility.

summary.para: Compresses and optionally encrypts items in persistent storage.

Compresses stored items using GZip compression to reduce storage size. Optionally encrypts compressed data using AES-256 encryption with PBKDF2 key derivation. The compressed item is saved with .gz extension (or .gz.enc if encrypted). Supports in-place compression or creating a new compressed copy. Ideal for archiving large datasets or reducing storage costs with security.

example.para: Compresses largefile.json to largefile.json.gz and removes original.
Compresses and encrypts sensitive.json with password protection.
Compresses all log files, keeping originals.
Compresses data.json to specific destination.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CompressPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Name
member.summary: The name/key of the item to compress.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CompressPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.DestinationName
member.summary: Destination name for compressed item. If not specified, adds .gz extension to original name. If password is used, adds .gz.enc extension.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CompressPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Password
member.summary: Password for AES-256 encryption. If specified, compressed data will be encrypted. Use Read-Host -AsSecureString to securely prompt for password.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CompressPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.CompressionLevel
member.summary: Compression level: Optimal (default), Fastest, or NoCompression.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CompressPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.KeepOriginal
member.summary: If specified, keeps the original item after compression.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CompressPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Force
member.summary: If specified, overwrites existing compressed item without confirmation.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CompressPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.PassThru
member.summary: If specified, returns information about the compressed item.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.CompressPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.DetermineDestinationName
member.summary: Determines the destination name based on parameters and encryption status.

summary.para: Connects to a data repository (Azure Blob, Queue, Service Bus, Key Vault, Disk, or InMemory).

Establishes a session to a data repository provider. The provider type is automatically detected from the supplied parameters (connection string, account name, vault name, etc.). Supports 12 authentication methods including connection strings, SAS tokens, Managed Identity, client secrets/certificates, interactive browser, device code, and DefaultAzureCredential. For local development, use -Disk or -InMemory switches which require no authentication.

example.para: Connects to a local disk-based repository at the specified path.
Connects to a volatile in-memory repository (useful for testing).
Connects to Azure Blob Storage using a connection string.
Connects to Azure Queue Storage using DefaultAzureCredential.
Connects to Azure Key Vault using Managed Identity.
Connects to an Azure Service Bus topic with a subscription.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.ConnectPSDataRepositoryCommand.DetermineProvider
member.summary: Determines the effective provider from cmdlet parameters. Delegates complex detection logic to ProviderDetector class. Passes WriteWarning callback for connection string validation warnings.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.ConnectPSDataRepositoryCommand.ValidateAuthMethodForProvider
member.summary: Validates that the current authentication method is supported by the detected provider. Delegates validation logic to ProviderDetector class.

member.summary: Validates that a required parameter is not null or empty.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.ConnectPSDataRepositoryCommand.TestRepositoryConnection
member.summary: Tests the repository connection by performing a simple operation. For Storage: checks if container exists or can be accessed. For Messaging: verifies session is valid.

summary.para: Deserializes a raw message string into a PowerShell object.

Converts a raw message string (e.g. received from an Azure Function Queue trigger) into a deserialized object using the module's Formatter. Does not require an active session or queue connection. Automatically detects the serialization format (JSON, XML, CSV) unless -Format is specified. If the message was sent with -IncludeMetadata, the metadata envelope is automatically unwrapped and the inner content is deserialized. Use -IncludeMetadata to attach the envelope metadata as note properties on the output object.

example.para: Deserializes a message received from an Azure Function Queue trigger.
Explicitly deserializes a JSON message from pipeline input.
Deserializes a message and includes envelope metadata (TypeName, Timestamp, etc.) as note properties.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ConvertFromPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.InputObject
member.summary: The raw message string to deserialize. Accepts pipeline input.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ConvertFromPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.Format
member.summary: Deserialization format. If not specified, attempts auto-detection based on content.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ConvertFromPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.IncludeMetadata
member.summary: If specified, includes metadata properties from the envelope (TypeName, Timestamp, etc.) as note properties on the deserialized object. Only applies when the message contains a metadata envelope (sent with -IncludeMetadata).

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.ConvertFromPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.TryUnwrapEnvelope(System.String,System.String@,System.Nullable{System.Text.Json.JsonElement}@,System.Nullable{PSDataRepository.Serialization.FormatType}@)
member.summary: Attempts to detect and unwrap a metadata envelope created by Send-PSDataRepositoryMessage -IncludeMetadata.

summary.para: Disconnects from the current data repository session.

Closes the active repository connection established by Connect-PSDataRepository. Disposes all underlying resources (storage clients, queue clients, cached connections) and clears the session state. Returns $true on success, $false if an error occurred during cleanup. Safe to call even when no session is active.

example.para: Disconnects from the current repository and releases all resources.
Typical connect-work-disconnect workflow.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.DisconnectPSDataRepositoryCommand.ProcessRecord
member.summary: Disconnects from the currently active repository and clears the session.

summary.para: Decompresses and optionally decrypts items in persistent storage.

Decompresses GZip-compressed items stored in the repository. Automatically decrypts items encrypted with Compress-PSDataRepositoryItem if password is provided. Automatically detects .gz extension and removes it from decompressed item name. Supports in-place decompression or creating a new decompressed copy. Ideal for restoring archived datasets or processing compressed/encrypted data.

example.para: Decompresses largefile.json.gz to largefile.json and removes compressed file.
Decrypts and decompresses encrypted file with password.
Decompresses all .gz files, keeping compressed originals.
Decompresses to specific destination.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ExpandPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Name
member.summary: The name/key of the compressed item to expand.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ExpandPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.DestinationName
member.summary: Destination name for decompressed item. If not specified, removes .gz extension from original name.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ExpandPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Password
member.summary: Password for AES-256 decryption. Required if item was encrypted with Compress-PSDataRepositoryItem. Use Read-Host -AsSecureString to securely prompt for password.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ExpandPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.KeepOriginal
member.summary: If specified, keeps the compressed item after decompression.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ExpandPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Force
member.summary: If specified, overwrites existing decompressed item without confirmation.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ExpandPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.PassThru
member.summary: If specified, returns information about the decompressed item.

summary.para: Retrieves items from persistent storage (Blob, Disk).

Loads and deserializes items from the connected repository (Azure Blob Storage, Disk). Supports retrieving single items, multiple items by pattern, or listing all items. Items are automatically deserialized based on format (JSON, XML, CSV) or content detection. Ideal for loading configuration, datasets, or processing results.

example.para: Retrieves and deserializes config.json.
Retrieves all JSON files from the data folder.
Explicitly deserializes as XML.
Returns raw content without deserialization.

summary.para: Lists all supported PSDataRepository providers and their capabilities.

Displays information about each data repository provider the module can work with, including provider type, supported capabilities (Storage, Queue, Secrets), authentication methods, and connection examples. Does not require an active session.

example.para: Lists all available providers with their capabilities.
Shows details for a specific provider.
Lists only providers that support queue messaging.

member.summary: Filter by provider name. If not specified, all providers are listed.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryProviderCommand.Capability
member.summary: Filter by capability (Storage, Queue, Secrets).

summary.para: Retrieves a secret value from the connected repository.
Gets the value of a secret from the connected repository (typically Azure Key Vault or FileSystem). Fails if the repository type does not support secrets or is not connected.

member.summary: The name of the secret to retrieve.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositorySecretCommand.ProcessRecord
member.summary: Processes the cmdlet and retrieves the specified secret from the repository.

summary.para: Retrieves information about the current repository session.

Displays details about the active PSDataRepository session including: - Connection status - Provider type (AzureBlob, AzureQueue, Disk, InMemory, etc.) - Authentication mode - Repository identity/endpoint - Session creation time Useful for debugging connectivity issues or verifying session configuration.

example.para: Displays current session information.
Shows detailed session information in list format.

summary.para: Receives and deserializes messages from the connected queue repository.

Retrieves messages from the active queue repository and deserializes them back to objects. Supports batching for efficient retrieval of large message volumes. Can operate in single-message mode, batch mode, or continuous polling mode. Messages are automatically acknowledged/deleted after successful deserialization by default.

example.para: Receives up to 10 messages and returns them as objects.
Continuously polls for messages, retrieving up to 100 per batch with 5-second delay between polls.
Peeks at messages without removing them from the queue.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.MaxMessages
member.summary: Maximum number of messages to receive in one call. Default: 1.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.VisibilityTimeoutSeconds
member.summary: Visibility timeout for received messages (how long they remain invisible to other consumers). Default: 30 seconds.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.Format
member.summary: Deserialization format. If not specified, attempts auto-detection based on content.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.Peek
member.summary: If specified, peeks messages without removing them from the queue (read-only).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.Raw
member.summary: If specified, returns raw message content without deserialization.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.NoAutoDelete
member.summary: If specified, does not automatically delete/acknowledge messages after processing. Useful for manual acknowledgement scenarios or debugging.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.Continuous
member.summary: If specified, continues polling continuously until interrupted (Ctrl+C).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.DelaySeconds
member.summary: Delay in seconds between continuous polling attempts. Default: 5 seconds. Only used with -Continuous.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.MaxIterations
member.summary: Maximum number of continuous polling iterations. Default: unlimited (until Ctrl+C). Only used with -Continuous.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.ContinueOnError
member.summary: If specified, continues processing even if individual message deserialization fails. Failed messages are written as errors but don't stop processing.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.MessageId
member.summary: The ID of a specific message to retrieve. When specified, only the message with the matching ID is returned (using peek, without removing from queue).

summary.para: Removes items from persistent storage (Blob, Disk).
Deletes items from the connected repository (Azure Blob Storage, Disk). Supports removing single or multiple items by name.

example.para: Removes temp.json from storage.
Removes all JSON files in backup folder without confirmation.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.RemovePSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Name
member.summary: The name/key of the item(s) to remove.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.RemovePSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Force
member.summary: If specified, skips confirmation prompts.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.RemovePSDataRepositoryItemCommand.ContinueOnError
member.summary: If specified, continues processing even if individual object deletion fails.

summary.para: Removes/deletes messages from the queue.

Deletes messages from the connected queue repository (InMemory, Disk, Azure Queue, Service Bus). Typically used after successful message processing to prevent reprocessing. Messages must have been received first (to obtain receipt handles). For Azure providers, uses receipt handle for deletion. For Disk/InMemory, uses message ID.

example.para: Receives and then deletes a message.
Receives and deletes multiple messages via pipeline.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.RemovePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.Message
member.summary: The message object(s) to delete. Typically obtained from Receive-PSDataRepositoryMessage.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.RemovePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.InputObject
member.summary: Pipeline input from Receive-PSDataRepositoryMessage (PSObject with __QueueMessage property).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.RemovePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.Force
member.summary: If specified, suppresses confirmation prompts.

summary.para: Deletes a secret from the connected repository.
Removes (deletes) a secret from the connected repository (typically Azure Key Vault or FileSystem). Fails if the repository type does not support secrets or is not connected.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.RemovePSDataRepositorySecretCommand.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the secret to remove.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.RemovePSDataRepositorySecretCommand.Force
member.summary: If specified, skips confirmation prompts.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.RemovePSDataRepositorySecretCommand.ProcessRecord
member.summary: Removes the specified secret from the connected repository.

summary.para: Sends objects to the connected queue repository.

Serializes and sends objects to the active queue repository (InMemory, Disk, Azure Queue, Service Bus). Supports pipeline input for processing large volumes of objects efficiently. Objects are serialized to JSON by default, with configurable format (XML, CSV). Uses batching for optimal performance (default batch size: 100 messages).

example.code: $tasks = @([PSCustomObject]@{ Id = 1; Action = "Backup"; Target = "DB01" }; [PSCustomObject]@{ Id = 2; Action = "Restart"; Target = "App02" }) $tasks | Send-PSDataRepositoryMessage
example.para: Sends a collection of objects to the queue.
Sends 100,000 custom objects with batching of 500 messages.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.InputObject
member.summary: The object(s) to send. Accepts pipeline input.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.Format
member.summary: Serialization format: Json (default), Xml, or Csv.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.BatchSize
member.summary: Batch size for sending messages. Default: 100. Larger batches improve throughput but use more memory.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.MaxDepth
member.summary: Maximum serialization depth to prevent infinite recursion. Default: 10.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.CsvDelimiter
member.summary: CSV delimiter (only used if Format = Csv). Default: comma (,).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.XmlRootName
member.summary: XML root element name (only used if Format = Xml). If not specified (null), automatically inferred from object type: - Single object: type name (e.g., 'Process') - Collection: type name + 'List' (e.g., 'ProcessList') Default: null (auto-detect).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.Force
member.summary: If specified, skips confirmation prompts.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.IncludeMetadata
member.summary: If specified, includes metadata envelope with type information and timestamp.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.IncludeMachineInfo
member.summary: If specified, includes machine name and user name in the metadata envelope. Only used with -IncludeMetadata. Disabled by default to prevent information disclosure.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.ContinueOnError
member.summary: If specified, continues processing even if individual message serialization fails. Failed messages are written as errors but don't stop the pipeline.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.SerializeObject(System.Management.Automation.PSObject)
member.summary: Serializes an object using the configured format.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.CreateMetadataEnvelope(System.Management.Automation.PSObject,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a metadata envelope around the serialized content.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.FlushBatch
member.summary: Flushes the current batch buffer to the queue repository.

summary.para: Saves items to persistent storage (Blob, Disk).

Serializes and stores items in the connected repository (Azure Blob Storage, Disk). Supports pipeline input for batch processing and multiple serialization formats. Items are stored as individual files/blobs with specified names. Ideal for saving configuration, datasets, or processing results.

example.code: $data = @([PSCustomObject]@{ Id = 1; Name = "Server01"; Status = "Running" }; [PSCustomObject]@{ Id = 2; Name = "Server02"; Status = "Stopped" }) $data | Set-PSDataRepositoryItem -Name "servers.json"
example.para: Saves a collection of objects as JSON to storage.
Saves configuration hashtable as JSON.
Saves each file item with unique name based on FullName property.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.InputObject
member.summary: The object to save. Accepts pipeline input.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Name

member.summary: XML root element name. Only used when Format = Xml. If not specified (null), automatically inferred from object type: - Single object: type name (e.g., 'Process') - Collection: type name + 'List' (e.g., 'ProcessList') Default: null (auto-detect).

member.summary: If specified, overwrites existing objects without confirmation.

member.summary: If specified, accumulates all pipeline objects and saves them as a collection (array). Useful for creating single JSON/XML/CSV file from multiple pipeline objects. Without this, each pipeline object is saved separately (potentially overwriting the same file).

Sets (creates or updates) a secret in the connected repository. Supports either plain text or secure string as the secret value. Supported repositories: AzureKeyVault, FileSystem.

summary.para: Tests the connectivity to the current PSDataRepository provider.

Validates that the current session's connection to the underlying provider is healthy. For storage providers (Azure Blob, Disk), attempts a lightweight existence probe. For queue providers (Azure Queue, Azure Service Bus, InMemory), attempts to query message count. Returns $true if the connection is healthy, $false otherwise.

example.para: Tests the current connection and returns $true or $false.
Reconnects if the connection is unhealthy.

summary.para: Tests if an object exists in the repository.
Checks whether one or more objects exist in the connected repository (Azure Blob Storage, Disk). Returns $true if exists, $false otherwise.

example.para: Returns $true if config.json exists, $false otherwise.
Conditional execution based on existence.

member.summary: The name/key of the object to test.

member.name: T:PSDataRepository.Common.CryptoHelper
member.summary:
Provides AES-256 encryption and decryption with PBKDF2 key derivation. Shared by Compress-PSDataRepositoryItem and Expand-PSDataRepositoryItem cmdlets.
V2 format (AES-GCM, default): [PSDR-ENC-V2 11B][Salt 32B][Nonce 12B][Tag 16B][Ciphertext]
V1 format (AES-CBC, legacy read-only): [PSDR-ENC-V1 11B][Salt 32B][IV 16B][Ciphertext]

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Common.CryptoHelper.HasEncryptionHeader(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Checks whether the data starts with any known encryption header (V1 or V2).

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Common.CryptoHelper.Encrypt(System.Byte[],System.Security.SecureString)
member.summary: Encrypts data using AES-256-GCM (V2) with a PBKDF2-derived key. Provides authenticated encryption — ciphertext integrity is verified on decryption.
member.returns: Encrypted payload including V2 header, salt, nonce, tag, and ciphertext.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Common.CryptoHelper.Decrypt(System.Byte[],System.Security.SecureString)
member.summary: Decrypts data encrypted by . Supports both V2 (AES-GCM) and V1 (AES-CBC, legacy) formats.
member.param: Encrypted payload including header, salt, and ciphertext.
Password used for key derivation (must match encryption).
member.returns: Decrypted plaintext bytes.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Common.CryptoHelper.DecryptV2(System.Byte[],System.Security.SecureString)
member.summary: Decrypts V2 (AES-GCM) payload. Format: [PSDR-ENC-V2 11B][Salt 32B][Nonce 12B][Tag 16B][Ciphertext]

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Common.CryptoHelper.DecryptV1(System.Byte[],System.Security.SecureString)
member.summary: Decrypts V1 (AES-CBC) payload. Retained for backward compatibility with existing encrypted data. Format: [PSDR-ENC-V1 11B][Salt 32B][IV 16B][Ciphertext]

member.name: T:PSDataRepository.Common.EncryptionConstants
member.summary: Shared encryption constants used by Compress and Expand cmdlets. Centralizing these ensures Compress and Expand always use matching parameters, preventing data loss from accidental desynchronization.

member.summary: AES key size in bits (256-bit / 32 bytes).

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Common.EncryptionConstants.AesBlockSize
member.summary: AES block size in bits (128-bit / 16 bytes).

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Common.EncryptionConstants.SaltSize
member.summary: Salt size in bytes for PBKDF2 key derivation (256 bits).

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Common.EncryptionConstants.IVSize
member.summary: IV size in bytes for AES-CBC mode (V1 only).

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Common.EncryptionConstants.Pbkdf2Iterations
member.summary: Number of PBKDF2 iterations for key derivation. OWASP 2023 recommends 600,000 for SHA-256. Current value is retained for backward compatibility with existing encrypted data. Consider migrating to 600,000 with a re-encryption strategy for new data.

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Common.EncryptionConstants.EncryptionMagicHeader
member.summary: Magic header bytes for V1 (AES-CBC) encrypted files.

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Common.EncryptionConstants.EncryptionMagicHeaderV2
member.summary: Magic header bytes for V2 (AES-GCM) encrypted files.

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Common.EncryptionConstants.GcmNonceSize
member.summary: AES-GCM nonce size in bytes (96 bits, NIST recommendation).

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Common.EncryptionConstants.GcmTagSize
member.summary: AES-GCM authentication tag size in bytes (128 bits).

member.name: T:PSDataRepository.Common.SecurityHelper
member.summary: Security helper for sanitizing sensitive information before logging.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Common.SecurityHelper.SensitiveKeysRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
(AccountKey|SharedAccessKey|SharedAccessSignature|sig)=([^;]+)
Options:
RegexOptions.IgnoreCase
Explanation:
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Aa].
○ Match a character in the set [Cc] exactly 2 times.
○ Match a character in the set [Oo].
○ Match a character in the set [Uu].
○ Match a character in the set [Nn].
○ Match a character in the set [Tt].
○ Match a character in the set [Kk\u212A].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a character in the set [Yy].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Ss].
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Hh].
○ Match a character in the set [Aa].
○ Match a character in the set [Rr].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a character in the set [Dd].
○ Match a character in the set [Aa].
○ Match a character in the set [Cc] exactly 2 times.
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Ss] exactly 2 times.
○ Match a character in the set [Kk\u212A].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a character in the set [Yy].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Ss] exactly 3 times.
○ Match a character in the set [Ii].
○ Match a character in the set [Gg].
○ Match a character in the set [Nn].
○ Match a character in the set [Aa].
○ Match a character in the set [Tt].
○ Match a character in the set [Uu].
○ Match a character in the set [Rr].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Ii].
○ Match a character in the set [Gg].
○ Match '='.
○ 2nd capture group.
○ Match a character other than ';' atomically at least once.

member.summary: Peeks a single message by its ID without removing it from the queue. Note: Azure Queue Storage does not support direct ID-based lookup. This peeks up to 32 messages (Azure API limit) and filters by ID.
member.param: The ID of the message to find.
member.returns: The matching message, or null if not found within the peek window.

member.name: T:PSDataRepository.Messaging.Providers.AzureServiceBusRepository
member.summary: Azure Service Bus implementation using Azure SDK directly. Supports both queues and topics with subscriptions. Supports all authentication modes: ConnectionString, ClientSecret, Certificate, ManagedIdentity, etc. Alternative to FluentStorage implementation with full authentication support.

member.summary: Internal constructor for unit testing — accepts pre-configured Azure SDK clients.

member.summary: Internal batch send logic that handles splitting into multiple batches if needed.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Messaging.Providers.AzureServiceBusRepository.PeekByIdAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Peeks a single message by its ID without removing it from the queue. Note: Azure Service Bus does not support direct MessageId-based peek. This peeks messages in batches and filters by MessageId. For large queues, the message may not be found if it is beyond the peek window.
member.param: The MessageId of the message to find.
member.returns: The matching message, or null if not found within the peek window.

member.summary: Asynchronously disposes all Azure Service Bus resources.

member.summary: Disk-based queue using directory-backed storage. Good for local/dev environments and simple persistence.

member.returns: The matching message, or null if not found.

member.summary: Disposes resources including the internal semaphore.

member.name: T:PSDataRepository.Messaging.Providers.InMemoryQueueRepository
member.summary: In-memory queue with configurable capacity limits. Ideal for testing and development scenarios where persistence is not required. Thread-safe implementation using ConcurrentQueue.

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Messaging.Providers.InMemoryQueueRepository.DefaultMaxCapacity
member.summary: Default maximum capacity for in-memory queue.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Messaging.Providers.InMemoryQueueRepository.#ctor(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: Optional queue name. Defaults to "memory-queue" if not specified.
Maximum number of messages the queue can hold. Default: 10000.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Messaging.Providers.InMemoryQueueRepository.SendAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Sends a single message to the queue asynchronously.
member.exception: Thrown when message is empty or whitespace.
Thrown when queue is at max capacity.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Messaging.Providers.InMemoryQueueRepository.SendBatchAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Sends a batch of messages to the queue asynchronously. Empty or whitespace messages are automatically filtered out.
member.exception: Thrown when messages collection is null.
Thrown when batch would exceed max capacity.

member.summary: Receives a single message from the queue.
member.param: Visibility timeout (not used in memory implementation).
member.returns: The received message, or null if queue is empty.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Messaging.Providers.InMemoryQueueRepository.ReceiveBatchAsync(System.Int32,System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Receives a batch of messages from the queue. Messages are dequeued immediately (no visibility timeout in memory implementation).
member.param: Maximum number of messages to receive.
Visibility timeout (not used in memory implementation).
member.returns: Collection of received messages (up to maxMessages count).

member.returns: Collection of peeked messages (without modifying queue).

member.summary: Peeks a single message by its ID without removing it from the queue.

member.returns: The matching message, or null if not found.

member.name: T:PSDataRepository.Serialization.FormatDetector
member.summary: Provides centralized format detection logic used by storage and messaging cmdlets. Detects from file extension and/or content heuristics.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.FormatDetector.DetectFromNameAndContent(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Detects format from file name extension first, then falls back to content heuristics. Used by storage cmdlets that have both a file name and content available.
member.param: The file/blob name (used for extension-based detection).
The content string (used for content-based detection fallback).
member.returns: The detected .

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.FormatDetector.DetectFromContent(System.String)
member.summary: Detects format from content heuristics only. Used by messaging cmdlets where no file name is available.
member.returns: The detected . Defaults to .

member.name: T:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter
member.summary:
Provides multi-format (JSON, XML, CSV) serialization and deserialization with: - Cycle detection - Depth limitation (maxDepth) - Ignored PS* properties - Custom CSV delimiter and custom XML root element - For XML, uses __PSDR_TypeName to rename the element to the real type - For CSV, flattens property values and if it's not flat, stores "Nested(TypeName)".
Implementation is split across partial class files: - Formatter.cs — Public API, shared helpers, constants - Formatter.Json.cs — JSON serialization/deserialization - Formatter.Xml.cs — XML serialization/deserialization - Formatter.Csv.cs — CSV serialization/deserialization

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.DefaultMaxDepth
member.summary: The default maximum depth if not otherwise specified.

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.TypeNameKey
member.summary: Internal special key for storing the real .NET type name in a dictionary for XML element naming.

member.name: T:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.ReferenceEqualityComparer
member.summary: Reference equality comparer for cycle detection in object graphs.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.Serialize(System.Object,PSDataRepository.Serialization.FormatType)
member.summary: Serializes an object/collection into a string using the given format, with default CSV delimiter (','), default XML root ('Root'), and default maxDepth=10.
member.param: The object or collection to serialize.
The desired format (Json, Xml, or Csv).
member.returns: A string containing the serialized data.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.Serialize(System.Object,PSDataRepository.Serialization.FormatType,System.Char,System.String)
member.summary: Serializes an object/collection into a string using the given format, letting you specify the CSV delimiter, XML root, and uses maxDepth=10 by default.
member.param: The object or collection to serialize.
The desired format (Json, Xml, or Csv).
Which delimiter to use if CSV.
Which root element name to use if XML.
member.returns: A string containing the serialized data.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.Serialize(System.Object,PSDataRepository.Serialization.FormatType,System.Char,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Serializes an object/collection into a string using the given format, allowing custom CSV delimiter, custom XML root name, and a user-defined maxDepth.
member.param: The object or collection to serialize.
The desired format (Json, Xml, or Csv).
XML root element name. Use null for auto-detection from object type.
Maximum recursion depth for JSON or XML. For CSV it's used in partial flattening logic.
member.returns: A string containing the serialized data in the chosen format.
member.exception: If the chosen format is unknown.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.InferXmlRootName(System.Object)
member.summary: Infers an intelligent XML root element name based on object type. - Single object: uses type name (e.g., "Process") - Array/Collection: uses type name + "List" (e.g., "ProcessList") - PSObject: extracts base object type - Fallback: "Root" CRITICAL: Properly handles PSObject wrappers and distinguishes collections from single objects.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.GetSimpleTypeName(System.Object)
member.summary: Gets a simple, clean type name from an object.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.Deserialize(System.String,PSDataRepository.Serialization.FormatType)
member.summary: Deserializes a string in JSON, XML, or CSV format back into an object (PSObject, array, etc.). For CSV, uses ',' delimiter by default. For XML, a fallback approach with XmlSerializer(object).
member.returns: The deserialized object. For CSV, an array of PSObject is typical.
member.exception: If the format is not recognized.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.Deserialize(System.String,PSDataRepository.Serialization.FormatType,System.String)
member.summary: Deserializes a string using format + additional parameter (used for XML root or CSV delimiter).
member.param: CSV delimiter (char) or XML root name (string).
member.returns: Deserialized object.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.IsPrimitiveOrString(System.Object)
member.summary: Checks if the given object is a primitive/string/enum/decimal/datetime, etc.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.IsSerializableType(System.Type)
member.summary: Checks if a Type can be safely serialized by System.Text.Json. Returns false for types that cannot be serialized (IntPtr, SafeHandle, Delegate, etc.). CRITICAL: Prevents serialization errors with system objects like Process, FileInfo, etc.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.SerializeToCsv(System.Object,System.Char)
member.summary: Serializes an object or collection into a CSV string. ENHANCED: Flattens nested objects into columns with dot notation (e.g., Process.Name, Process.Id). Supports both single complex objects and collections.
member.param: The object or collection to serialize.
The delimiter to use for CSV columns.
member.returns: A CSV-formatted string representing the input.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.FlattenPropertiesForCsv(System.Management.Automation.PSObject,System.String,System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.Object},System.Collections.Generic.HashSet{System.Object},System.Int32)
member.summary: Recursively flattens properties into a dictionary with dot notation. Example: Process object → "Name", "Id", "Company.Name", etc. CRITICAL: Skips non-serializable types (IntPtr, SafeHandle) to prevent errors. ENHANCED: Includes circular reference detection to prevent infinite loops.
member.param: The current property path prefix (for dot notation).
Dictionary to store flattened key-value pairs.
Set of already visited objects to detect circular references.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.EscapeCsv(System.Object)
member.summary: Escapes a value for CSV formatting by always enclosing it in double quotes. Also escapes any double quotes in the content.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.IsFlatValue(System.Object)
member.summary: Checks if the value is flat enough to serialize into a CSV cell.
member.returns: True if flat, false if complex or nested.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.DeserializeFromCsv(System.String)
member.summary: Deserializes a CSV string into an array of PSObjects. Uses RFC 4180 compliant parsing for quoted fields.
member.returns: An array of PSObject instances representing each row.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.DeserializeFromCsvUsingDelimiter(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Deserializes CSV with a specified delimiter (passed as string, using first char).

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.DeserializeFromCsvWithDelimiter(System.String,System.Char)
member.summary: Core CSV deserialization with proper RFC 4180 parsing. Handles newlines within quoted fields.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.ParseCsvRecords(System.String,System.Char)
member.summary: Parses CSV content into records, handling multiline quoted fields. Returns list of string arrays (one per record).

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.InferCsvValue(System.String)
member.summary: Attempts to infer the actual type of a CSV value and convert it. Tries: null, bool, int, long, double, DateTime, then falls back to string.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.SerializeToJson(System.Object,System.Int32)
member.summary: Serializes an object to JSON using recursion/cycle protection and respecting maxDepth.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.DeserializeFromJson(System.String)
member.summary: Deserializes a JSON string into a PSObject or array or scalar values. CRITICAL: Includes error handling for malformed JSON with clear error messages.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.CleanObjectForJson(System.Object,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Collections.Generic.HashSet{System.Object})
member.summary: Recursively cleans an object for JSON. Removes cycles, limits depth, ignores PS* props, tries not to fail on getters. Uses persistent visited set per serialization call to properly detect cycles anywhere in the graph.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.CleanPsObjectForJson(System.Management.Automation.PSObject,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Collections.Generic.HashSet{System.Object})
member.summary: Cleans a PSObject, ignoring properties that start with PS, with recursion/cycle limit. CRITICAL: Catches exceptions from property getters to prevent serialization failures. CRITICAL: Filters out non-serializable types (IntPtr, SafeHandle, Delegate) before attempting serialization.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.CleanDictionaryForJson(System.Collections.IDictionary,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Collections.Generic.HashSet{System.Object})
member.summary: Cleans a dictionary by recursing over key-value entries. CRITICAL: Converts all keys to strings because JSON only supports string keys.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.CleanEnumerableForJson(System.Collections.IEnumerable,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Collections.Generic.HashSet{System.Object})
member.summary: Cleans an enumerable by building a list of cleaned items. CRITICAL: Handles null items and large collections safely.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.CleanUnknownObjectForJson(System.Object,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Collections.Generic.HashSet{System.Object})
member.summary: Cleans an unknown .NET object by enumerating its properties (wrapped in PSObject). CRITICAL: Catches exceptions from property getters to prevent serialization failures. CRITICAL: Filters out non-serializable types (IntPtr, SafeHandle, Delegate) before attempting serialization.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.ConvertJsonElement(System.Text.Json.JsonElement)
member.summary: Converts a JsonElement to a PSObject, array, or scalar for JSON deserialization.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.ConvertJsonObject(System.Text.Json.JsonElement)
member.summary: Converts a JSON object element to a PSObject by enumerating properties.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.ConvertJsonArray(System.Text.Json.JsonElement)
member.summary: Converts a JSON array element to an object[].

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.SerializeToXml(System.Object,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Serializes an object to XML with manual structure and cycle/recursion limit. If we detect a real .NET type, we store it in a dictionary as __PSDR_TypeName, so we can rename the element (e.g. <CmdletInfo>) instead of <Object>. CRITICAL: Removes __PSDR_TypeName from root dictionary to prevent duplicate element nesting.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.WriteXmlNode(System.Xml.XmlWriter,System.Object)
member.summary: Writes an already-cleaned object (dictionary, list, scalar) as XML. If dictionary has "__PSDR_TypeName", we use that as the element name.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.SafeXmlName(System.String)
member.summary: Sanitizes an XML element name by replacing invalid characters and ensuring it starts with a valid character.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.DeserializeFromXml(System.String)
member.summary: Deserializes XML using a fallback XmlSerializer(typeof(object)) approach. For precise control, you'd manually parse with XmlReader.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.CleanObjectForXml(System.Object,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Collections.Generic.HashSet{System.Object})
member.summary: Cleans the object for XML with cycle/recursion limit. Also, if it's a real .NET object, we store its type name into __PSDR_TypeName for usage in WriteXmlNode. Uses persistent visited set per serialization call to properly detect cycles.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.CleanPsObjectXml(System.Management.Automation.PSObject,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Collections.Generic.HashSet{System.Object})
member.summary: Cleans a PSObject for XML, ignoring PS* properties. If it's a real .NET object, we store its type name for usage in the final XML element.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.CleanDictionaryXml(System.Collections.IDictionary,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Collections.Generic.HashSet{System.Object})
member.summary: Cleans a dictionary by recursing over key-value entries for XML.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.CleanEnumerableXml(System.Collections.IEnumerable,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Collections.Generic.HashSet{System.Object})
member.summary: Cleans an enumerable by creating a list of cleaned items for XML.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Serialization.Formatter.CleanUnknownObjectXml(System.Object,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Collections.Generic.HashSet{System.Object})
member.summary: Cleans an unknown .NET object for XML, storing the real type name in __PSDR_TypeName so that final XML can rename the element accordingly.

member.name: T:PSDataRepository.Session.RepositorySession
member.summary: Holds the current active repository session, including repository instance, configuration options, and authentication details. Uses static fields because PowerShell cmdlets run single-threaded per runspace. AsyncLocal would lose context across Task.Run() calls used in sync-over-async pattern.

member.name: T:PSDataRepository.Storage.StorageOption
member.summary: Represents all options for connecting to a data repository. Includes logical identifiers and authentication configuration.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Storage.StorageOption.RepositoryIdentity
member.summary: Gets or sets the logical repository identity. Typically represents the account, organization, tenant, or workspace. Examples: Azure Storage Account name, KeyVault name, or file system path.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Storage.StorageOption.RepositoryNamespace
member.summary: Gets or sets the logical namespace or container within the repository. Universal property used for: - Container name (Blob Storage) - Queue name (Azure Queue, Service Bus Queue) - Topic name (Service Bus Topic) - Folder/vault name (KeyVault, file system) This replaces the deprecated ContainerName, QueueName, and TopicName properties.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Storage.StorageOption.TopicName
member.summary: Gets or sets the topic name (for Service Bus topics). Used only when distinguishing between queue and topic scenarios. Applicable only to provider.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Storage.StorageOption.SubscriptionName
member.summary: Gets or sets the subscription name (for Service Bus topic subscriptions). Required when using Service Bus topics. Applicable only to provider.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Storage.StorageOption.ProviderName
member.summary: Gets or sets the provider name (e.g., "AzureBlob", "AzureQueue", "AzureServiceBus").

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Storage.StorageOption.Authentication
member.summary: Gets or sets the options specifying how to authenticate to this repository.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Storage.StorageOption.Encoding
member.summary: Gets or sets the text encoding for file/blob operations (optional). Defaults to UTF8 if not specified. Applicable only to and providers.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Storage.StorageOption.Validate
member.summary: Validates that the options are consistent for the configured provider. Throws if inconsistencies are detected.
